SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.rupture

Class PipeFireRuptureMaterial

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.rupture.PipeFireRuptureMaterial
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class PipeFireRuptureMaterial extends Object implements Serializable
Temperature-dependent material properties for blowdown pipe fire-rupture strain-rate studies.

The curve stores the material properties used by the legacy strain-rate workbook: heat capacity, thermal conductivity, ultimate tensile strength, strain-rate multiplier, rupture strain limit, steel density, and Sellars-Tegart creep/strain constants. Temperatures are represented in degrees Celsius to align with material-certificate and spreadsheet tables.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • PipeFireRuptureMaterial

      public PipeFireRuptureMaterial(String materialName, double[] temperatureC, double[] heatCapacityJPerKgK, double[] thermalConductivityWPerMK, double[] ultimateTensileStrengthMPa, double[] strainEffectFactor, double[] ruptureStrainLimit, double densityKgPerM3, double sellarsTegartA, double sellarsTegartAlpha, double sellarsTegartN, double activationTemperatureK, double correctionStrainRatePerMinute, double correctionLimitTemperatureC, String dataSource)
      Creates a material curve for pipe fire-rupture strain-rate calculations.
      Parameters:
      materialName - material name; must not be empty
      temperatureC - temperature grid in degrees Celsius; must be strictly increasing
      heatCapacityJPerKgK - heat capacity values in J/kg-K; must match the temperature grid
      thermalConductivityWPerMK - thermal conductivity values in W/m-K; must match the grid
      ultimateTensileStrengthMPa - UTS values in MPa; must match the grid
      strainEffectFactor - strain-rate multiplier values; must match the grid
      ruptureStrainLimit - limiting accumulated strain values; must match the grid
      densityKgPerM3 - material density in kg/m3; must be positive
      sellarsTegartA - Sellars-Tegart pre-exponential constant; must be positive
      sellarsTegartAlpha - Sellars-Tegart alpha constant in 1/MPa; must be positive
      sellarsTegartN - Sellars-Tegart exponent; must be positive
      activationTemperatureK - activation term Q/R in K; must be positive
      correctionStrainRatePerMinute - strain rate used to correct extrapolated temperatures; must be positive
      correctionLimitTemperatureC - temperature above which the correction factor is set to one
      dataSource - description of data source or assumption basis
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the material curve is invalid

      public static PipeFireRuptureMaterial fromSpreadsheetMaterialName(String materialName)
      Selects a built-in spreadsheet material curve from a material name.
      Parameters:
      materialName - material name such as 22Cr duplex, SS 316, or CS 235
      Returns:
      matching material curve
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if no built-in curve matches the name

      private static double interpolate(double value, double[] xValues, double[] yValues)
      Performs linear interpolation with endpoint clamping.
      Parameters:
      value - requested x value
      xValues - increasing x grid
      yValues - y values corresponding to the x grid
      Returns:
      interpolated y value

    • validateCurves

      private static void validateCurves(double[] temperatureC, double[] heatCapacityJPerKgK, double[] thermalConductivityWPerMK, double[] ultimateTensileStrengthMPa, double[] strainEffectFactor, double[] ruptureStrainLimit)
      Validates curve arrays.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - temperature grid
      heatCapacityJPerKgK - heat capacity values
      thermalConductivityWPerMK - thermal conductivity values
      ultimateTensileStrengthMPa - ultimate tensile strength values
      strainEffectFactor - strain-effect values
      ruptureStrainLimit - rupture strain limit values
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any array is invalid

      Validates the temperature grid.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - temperature grid
      Returns:
      validated grid length
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the grid is invalid

      values - curve values
      expectedLength - expected array length
      name - parameter name for messages
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the curve is invalid
